subject: Re: How is tomostar wrpAxisAngle generated?
body:
To be more precise, the exact behavior is:
tilt axis angle = 85.2 degrees
calculated = 94.8 degrees

(180-94.8 = 85.2)
On Monday, July 3, 2023 at 12:12:19 AM UTC+2 joe dobbs wrote:

> Dear Alister,
>
> Thanks for the information and suggestions. That's exactly the case, the 
> calculated angle differs by exactly 180 degrees. Any tips for tricking warp 
> into getting the right one, or do you think it would be a bug that would 
> need more of a "software update" kind of fix?
>
> Thanks again,
> Joe
>
> On Monday, July 3, 2023 at 12:04:47 AM UTC+2 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Joe,
>>
>> Does anyone know how wrpAxisAngle in the tomostar file is generated? From 
>> the github it looks like some transformation of the offset coordinates. I 
>> suppose we would expect it to come out to be the same as the tilt axis 
>> offset?
>>
>>
>> wrpAxisAngle is the orientation of the tilt-axis within the tilt images, 
>> it’s calculated here 
>> <https://github.com/cramerlab/warp/blob/21e804c6b362154dae6177f381e48198e599df2c/Warp/Controls/TaskDialogs/Tomo/DialogTomoImportImod.xaml.cs#L268-L296> from 
>> the parts of the IMOD xf data which encode the 2D image rotation (first 
>> four columns).
>>
>> I have some data where the warp reconstructions differ substantially from 
>> reconstructions made in etomo (with the same xf) and they have a different 
>> wrpAxisAngle in the tomostars than the known tilt axis angle, where I have 
>> seen in the past they've been the same, and I wonder if this could be where 
>> my reconstructions are going wrong.
>>
>>
>> I’m assuming the number you have is your actual tilt axis angle +- ~180 
>> degrees? The angle is calculated in Warp by decomposing a 3x3 rotation 
>> matrix into Euler angles and taking one of those angles - because there can 
>> be multiple valid sets of Euler angles for a given matrix it’s likely this 
>> is where the error is occurring. If this is the case it’s a bug and would 
>> need to be fixed within Warp.
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>  
>>
>> On 2 Jul 2023, at 16:53, joe dobbs <dobbsj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> Hello,
>>
>> Does anyone know how wrpAxisAngle in the tomostar file is generated? From 
>> the github it looks like some transformation of the offset coordinates. I 
>> suppose we would expect it to come out to be the same as the tilt axis 
>> offset?
>>
>> I have some data where the warp reconstructions differ substantially from 
>> reconstructions made in etomo (with the same xf) and they have a different 
>> wrpAxisAngle in the tomostars than the known tilt axis angle, where I have 
>> seen in the past they've been the same, and I wonder if this could be where 
>> my reconstructions are going wrong.
>>
>>
>> Best,
>> Joe.
>>
>> --

>> .
>>
>>
>>
